catalysts_general: Discovery: Broadcom is still the best non-Nvidia chip-and-networking lever on AI clusters after March earnings showed AI semiconductor revenue of $8.4 billion, up 106% year over year, with Q2 AI revenue guided to $10.7 billion and fresh April evidence of TPU-related demand from Anthropic and Google.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Primary filings still show accelerating AI custom-silicon and networking demand with exceptional cash generation, though the stock is a quality AI infrastructure compounder rather than a true bargain.
Microsoft is a high-quality AI and cloud compounder trading roughly one-third below its 52-week high, with Azure demand still strong and near-term April-May catalysts that can support a rerating.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Salesforce has the cleanest large-cap AI monetization proof point in enterprise software after reporting Agentforce ARR of $800 million, over 29,000 deals, and more than 60% of Q4 Agentforce/Data 360 bookings coming from existing-customer expansion.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Salesforce is a beaten-down large-cap software name with real AI monetization proof, massive free cash flow, and an active buyback, but the May 27, 2026 earnings report still needs to confirm that Agentforce can drive organic reacceleration.

Post-selloff Intuit looks mispriced for a high-margin, cash-generative platform with reiterated double-digit growth, a live tax-season catalyst, spring 2026 AI rollouts, and an accelerated buyback underpinning the downside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron sits at the center of the HBM upcycle after putting HBM4 for NVIDIA Vera Rubin into high-volume production in mid-March while industry trackers continue to describe 2026 HBM supply as effectively sold out.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron is executing into an AI-memory supercycle with record fiscal Q2 results, HBM4 already in high-volume production for NVIDIA Vera Rubin, and late-June earnings as the next major proof point, but the stock is not deeply discounted and insider flows are neutral-to-negative.

Qualys is a high-margin, cash-generative cybersecurity platform trading at a depressed multiple after a 50% drawdown, and the May 2026 print can rerate the stock if ETM and federal wins start offsetting the guided slowdown.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Oracle's March 10 fiscal Q3 2026 print showed cloud revenue up 44%, OCI up 84%, and RPO up 325% to $553 billion, which is rare proof that AI cloud demand is converting into multi-year backlog and financed capacity rather than just hype.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Oracle's AI cloud demand is translating into real OCI growth and an unusually large contracted backlog, while the stock is already discounting a severe capex and cash-flow air pocket.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Warby Parker has shifted from concept stock to plausible self-help story after delivering its first full-year profit, authorizing a buyback, and layering in new distribution and smart-glasses optionality.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Warby Parker has crossed into profitability, is generating free cash flow, and now has multiple 2026 catalysts in store expansion, Target shop-in-shops, and Google AI glasses, but the stock is still not cheap enough for a stronger call.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Impactive Capital said it beneficially owned 1,713,553 WEX shares, a 4.9% stake worth roughly $243 million against a cited $4.95 billion market cap, and escalated its campaign on March 30 with proxy materials attacking governance, capital allocation, and operational execution.
growth_picks.analyst_take: WEX screens as a cheap, high-margin cash compounder with an activist-driven governance catalyst, but muted organic growth and leverage keep it below top-tier conviction.

catalysts_general: Discovery: GitLab's board approved an inaugural $400 million share repurchase authorization on March 3, 2026 after fiscal-year revenue grew 26% to $955.2 million and adjusted free cash flow reached $219.6 million, making the authorization roughly 10% to 11% of the company's cited $3.5 billion to $3.9 billion market value.
growth_picks.analyst_take: GitLab is a high-margin DevSecOps platform trading about 64% below its 52-week high despite 23%-26% growth, strong free cash flow, and a $400 million buyback, but it still needs to prove AI monetization can offset slowing FY27 growth.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Benzinga's unusual-options page showed a bullish April 1 print in PDD's May 15, 2026 $115 calls for 9,900 contracts with volume/open-interest of 83 and just 39 days to expiry, a sizeable upside bet in a liquid large-cap ADR.
growth_picks.analyst_take: PDD is a cheap, cash-rich ecommerce compounder with double-digit revenue growth, but the rerating depends on proving Temu can absorb tariff friction without a lasting margin reset.

Accenture looks like a contrarian quality buy near its 52-week low because the stock is pricing in federal-spending and AI-disruption fears more harshly than the company’s still-strong bookings, cash generation and partner-led AI pipeline justify.
High-margin North American gold producer with visible self-funded growth through the Island Gold District expansion, supported by a near-term earnings catalyst and strong gold-price tailwinds.
CRDO is a profitable AI-connectivity winner with record growth, fresh product and IP catalysts, and a June earnings setup, but concentration and competition keep it below strong-buy status.
High-quality EDA leader with a real Synopsys-Ansys integration catalyst and resilient backlog, but still priced for execution while China and IP headwinds linger.
Cheap, cash-generative toy and IP owner with a recent CEO open-market buy and near-term earnings and entertainment catalysts, offset by soft demand and 2026 margin pressure.

Atlassian still combines 20%+ revenue growth with strong free cash flow and a stock near its 52-week low, but the next few weeks need to prove its AI and enterprise push can monetize faster than it pressures margins.

META remains a high-quality ad and AI compounder with strong cash generation and a reasonable forward multiple ahead of April 29 earnings, though capex escalation and antitrust risk keep it out of STRONG BUY territory.
Gen looks mispriced for its cash generation and raised FY26 outlook, but the stock still needs May earnings and continued deleveraging to prove the MoneyLion-led growth story is durable.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Activist Irenic Capital disclosed an economic interest in about 2.5% of Snap's Class A shares, roughly a $185 million position at Snap's cited $7.41 billion market cap, and publicly pushed AI monetization, cost cuts, governance changes, and buybacks while arguing for at least $26.37 per share of value.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Activist pressure plus improving ad and subscription monetization with positive free cash flow make SNAP a contrarian turnaround buy ahead of Q1 results, though founder control caps rerating speed.

Pinterest looks mispriced after a 55% drawdown given double-digit revenue and user growth, strong free cash flow, and near-term support from Elliott-backed repurchases and the tvScientific/AI monetization push.

Corsair looks mispriced at about 7.4x forward earnings and below book while still generating positive free cash flow; the setup is helped by a new $50 million buyback and elevated short interest, but weak 2026 revenue guidance and tariff/supply-chain risk keep conviction below high.

QCOM looks like a reasonably cheap cash-generating semiconductor compounder with near-term handset noise already reflected, while automotive, IoT and AI-PC diversification give the April 29, 2026 earnings report rerating potential.

Record profitability and fee-based growth, fresh loan-platform wins, and repeated CEO open-market buying support a rebound, though valuation and charge-off/accounting scrutiny keep this out of high-conviction territory.

KKR looks attractively priced versus its own growth profile after a 40% drawdown, with record 2025 fundraising/AUM growth, insider buying from top executives, and a near-term Q1 earnings catalyst, but private-credit stress and a high beta keep this below STRONG BUY.

Post-approval weakness leaves Rocket trading near cash-plus-PRV value while KRESLADI launch and restarted cardiology programs offer asymmetric upside, but dilution and execution risk keep it speculative.

Global Payments looks mispriced for a flat-growth processor because management guided to double-digit adjusted EPS growth, keeps generating strong free cash flow, and has visible rerating catalysts if the Worldpay combination executes.

FIS looks like a contrarian value buy after a 45% drawdown: forward valuation and free-cash-flow yield are cheap, and Tier 1-2 company disclosures point to a meaningful 2026 cash-flow lift from the Total Issuing integration, but elevated leverage and execution risk cap conviction.

Aptiv looks mispriced after the April 1 Versigent separation: the remaining core auto-tech business trades at a single-digit forward P/E with solid FCF, while May 5 earnings are a near-term test for margin durability and the post-spin rerating case.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Europe entered the April 2026 injection season with a fragile supply outlook while the Middle East conflict is lifting gas prices and cargo-diversion value, giving Cheniere immediate leverage through Corpus Christi Stage 3 ramp-up and U.S.-origin cargo security.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheniere combines cheap cash-flow-backed LNG infrastructure with near-term Stage 3 ramp and a May 7 earnings catalyst, though leverage and a stock already near highs cap upside.
Post-short-report selling has pushed ADMA into deep-value territory versus its reiterated 2026 growth outlook, but May earnings need to validate ASCENIV demand and working-capital quality.

Alkami looks like a beaten-down fintech compounder with strong recurring-revenue visibility, fresh MANTL-driven customer wins, and a notable March sponsor purchase, but slower 2026 growth and persistent execution risk keep conviction below high.

UiPath looks like a contrarian software value play with strong cash generation, a large new buyback, and credible agentic-automation catalysts, though competition and execution risk keep it out of strong-buy territory.

Customer trends are recovering after the late-2025 data incident and a large insider buy supports the setup, but 35.8x forward earnings and still-thin retail margins leave too little valuation cushion for a fresh BUY.
Cheap valuation and solid Crocs-brand cash generation are offset by unresolved HEYDUDE weakness, soft U.S. wholesale trends, and tariff risk ahead of the April 30, 2026 earnings report.
2027 Medicare Advantage rate relief and an April 29 earnings catalyst improve the setup, but Humana is still working through a 2026 star-ratings profit hole and medical-cost execution risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: NKE is down about 45% from its 52-week high and trading just above its low as Wall Street loses patience with Elliott Hill's reset; June 30, 2026 earnings are the clean catalyst for channel cleanup and demand stabilization, but China weakness and a slower-than-promised turnaround can still produce another guide-down.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Elite brand with early wholesale and North America stabilization, but the turnaround is still too early and China plus margin pressure keep the stock in prove-it mode.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: France's April 6 draft rearmament plan specifically prioritizes missile and drone stockpiles, and AeroVironment has direct exposure because Switchblade loitering munitions and counter-swarm systems are already deployed across multiple theaters and still carrying fresh production ceiling expansion.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Defense demand is real and backlog remains strong, but AVAV is still expensive after a major drawdown and the SCAR setback plus negative free cash flow keep the risk/reward from clearing a fresh BUY bar.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Nikesh Arora bought 68,085 shares for $9,999,977 on March 27, 2026, his first major discretionary open-market buy after years of sales, with PANW trading near 52-week lows and roughly 30% below its 52-week high.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality cybersecurity platform with a notable CEO open-market buy and near-term integration/product catalysts, but acquisition-driven margin pressure and a still-expensive valuation limit upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Estée Lauder remains deeply discounted after a brutal reset, but the latest earnings materials still point to organic sales growth, raised fiscal 2026 outlook, and ongoing restructuring-driven margin recovery.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Turnaround execution is improving, but EL still needs the May 1 fiscal Q3 print to prove margin recovery can outrun tariff and travel-retail headwinds.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Grocery Outlet stands out because multiple directors bought aggressively into a post-earnings collapse, creating one of the clearest recent cluster-buy signals in a $1B-plus retailer.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Heavy March insider buying and a 64% drawdown make Grocery Outlet a credible deep-value turnaround watchlist name, but management's own 2026 guide still points to negative-to-flat comps and a messy restructuring.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Vertiv remains a top picks-and-shovels beneficiary because February 2026 results showed organic orders growth of 252% and March channel checks pointed to further capacity expansion to serve liquid-cooling and power demand from AI data centers.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Vertiv has real AI data-center demand and a near-term earnings catalyst, but the stock is near highs, trading at a rich multiple, and offers little margin of safety after recent insider selling.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CSGP is down about 60% from its 52-week high and roughly 1% above its low as Homes.com spending wrecked near-term EPS; the April 28, 2026 earnings report, activist pressure, and the newly authorized $700 million buyback are concrete stop-the-bleeding catalysts, but if Homes.com monetization slips again the stock can stay dead money.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality core real estate data assets and clear near-term catalysts are offset by still-unproven Homes.com monetization and activist scrutiny.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: AVOID candidate: Duolingo's February 2026 guidance reset showed AI is helping usage but hurting monetization discipline, with management moving premium AI features down-market, 2026 bookings growth guided to only about 11%, and margins set to compress.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality, cash-generative platform is cheap after the collapse, but management's 2026 user-growth pivot still needs a clean Q1 re-acceleration in monetization before the stock deserves a fresh BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Axsome has a high-impact near-term catalyst with an April 30, 2026 FDA decision date for AXS-05 in Alzheimer's agitation layered on top of strong existing commercial growth.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Strong commercial growth and an April 30, 2026 FDA catalyst support the story, but the stock is near its high, the Alzheimer’s agitation filing carries execution risk, and there is not enough margin of safety to chase here.

catalysts_general: Discovery: GE Vernova remains one of the cleanest AI-power infrastructure beneficiaries as utilities and hyperscalers race to secure generation and grid equipment for data-center demand.
growth_picks.analyst_take: GE Vernova has genuine gas and grid backlog momentum tied to AI-driven power demand, but with shares near highs, wind losses still weighing on 2026, and valuation already rich, the risk/reward is not attractive enough for a fresh buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Kratos has fresh momentum from both an analyst re-rating and a new Pentagon-linked hypersonic-missile-defense contract, making it one of the cleaner near-term defense-news setups.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Real defense-growth catalysts and a larger backlog support the story, but KTOS still screens too expensive for its low margins, negative free cash flow, recent dilution, and ongoing insider selling.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Europe's March 26, 2026 air-defense scramble and France's April 6 missile-and-drone stockpile push support sustained demand for RTX missile franchises just as Raytheon is ramping AMRAAM, Tomahawk, SM-3 and SM-6 output under February 2026 Pentagon framework agreements.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality aerospace and defense franchise with backlog and missile demand support, but the stock is near highs and Pratt execution overhangs leave limited margin of safety.

catalysts_general: Discovery: The same March-April 2026 European air-defense shortage directly favors Lockheed because PAC-3 MSE and THAAD interceptor capacity is being expanded aggressively for U.S. and allied demand while Europe is localizing PAC-3 components.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Missile-defense demand and a near-term earnings catalyst support the business, but with shares near highs, limited implied upside, ongoing F-35/TR-3 execution risk, and no clear insider-buying signal, the setup is not cheap enough for a fresh BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: United Therapeutics authorized a new $2.0 billion buyback on March 9, 2026 and immediately front-loaded $1.5 billion through accelerated repurchase agreements with Citi, an authorization equal to roughly 8.5% of the company's cited $23.42 billion market cap.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality rare-disease franchise with fresh Tyvaso/IPF and organ-platform catalysts, but the stock is near its highs and still heavily exposed to treprostinil competition and concentration risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Lam is a strong second-order AI fab-buildout play because January and February updates made clear management is reorganizing for 'AI era' demand while customers push more complex memory, packaging, and wafer-fab spending tied to AI accelerators and HBM.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lam is executing well into AI, HBM, and advanced logic demand, but with shares at $258.76 near the 52-week high and only about 10% implied upside to the analyst target, it is a quality semiconductor equipment name at a poor entry rather than a fresh value buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Bausch + Lomb is interesting because nine insiders bought stock in a cluster after a difficult period, suggesting internal conviction that the eye-care franchise is stabilizing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Bausch + Lomb is executing better operationally with 2026 growth guidance, fresh surgical/product catalysts and notable insider buying, but leverage, Bausch Health's 88% control overhang and only modest valuation upside limit the setup.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ADBE is down about 46% from its 52-week high and sitting at a fresh low after the CEO succession shock and AI-disruption fears; the expected June 11, 2026 earnings report and the Adobe-NVIDIA partnership are the next real checkpoints, but if AI monetization still looks thin the low multiple can compress further.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Adobe is statistically cheap for a software franchise with 89% gross margins and strong cash generation, but the stock needs proof that AI products can deepen the moat rather than compress it while the CEO succession remains unresolved.

catalysts_general: Discovery: FedEx has a timely corporate-action catalyst because the company used its April 8 investor-day window to sharpen the case for the June 2026 FedEx Freight spin-off and earnings uplift.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The June 2026 FedEx Freight spin-off is a real catalyst, but with FDX near its 52-week high and Freight still absorbing weak-demand and separation-cost pressure, the stock lacks a clear margin of safety.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Washington's April 2-6, 2026 tightening of Section 232 treatment on copper derivatives should widen the U.S. copper premium, and Freeport is the cleanest large-cap domestic beneficiary because its North American system includes mines, a smelter and downstream rod exposure.
growth_picks.analyst_take: FCX has real long-term copper leverage and dated near-term catalysts, but with the stock near its 52-week high the upside is not compelling enough to offset unresolved Grasberg restart and shipment risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: The same April 2026 Section 232 tightening strengthens the case for domestic steel pricing power, and Steel Dynamics is a direct beneficiary because it is one of the largest U.S. steel producers with broad domestic mill and fabrication exposure.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Domestic steel pricing, fabrication backlog, and tariff tightening support near-term earnings, but with shares near their highs, negative recent FCF, and no open-market insider buying, the upside looks mostly priced in.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Bumble is a hated consumer-internet name, but the founder-led reset, hard cost actions, and renewed focus on higher-intent organic growth have created an early turnaround setup.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap founder-led turnaround with a near-term earnings catalyst, but steep paying-user declines and March insider-related selling keep Bumble in show-me mode.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Middle East shipping disruption is tightening tanker availability and changing voyage economics, which is directly positive for International Seaways because it operates spot-exposed crude and product tanker fleets that benefit from higher rates and longer ton-mile routes.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Tanker fundamentals and capital returns are strong, but INSW is already near its high and the current setup lacks the margin of safety and insider support needed for a fresh buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: PYPL is down about 42% from its 52-week high with an 8.5x trailing P/E and May 5, 2026 earnings ahead; Venmo global transfers, PYUSD expansion, and a refreshed board give management multiple ways to prove checkout acceleration, but if branded checkout growth still disappoints the stock may remain a value trap.
growth_picks.analyst_take: PayPal is cheap and cash-generative with a May 5 earnings catalyst, but weak 2026 guidance, checkout-share pressure, and leadership turnover keep the setup in prove-it mode.

Elite obesity and diabetes execution plus a deep incretin pipeline keep Lilly structurally strong, but the stock still trades at a premium while reimbursement cuts and oral competition threaten multiple expansion.

Expedia has strong cash generation and a fast-growing B2B engine, but U.S. travel softness, search-platform disintermediation risk, and no clear insider-buying signal keep the risk/reward only balanced ahead of May 2026 earnings.

Blackstone still has strong fundraising and realization momentum, but private-credit redemption stress and only moderate upside into the April 23 earnings update keep the risk/reward balanced rather than compelling.
High-quality Permian operator with real cash generation and active deleveraging, but commodity-price risk and recent large-holder selling limit near-term upside at current levels.

High-quality AI-memory interface and IP franchise with MRDIMM and client-chipset upside, but the stock still looks fully valued relative to growth and carries meaningful concentration and execution risk.

The $1.5 billion Çopler sale, active buyback, and Hod Maden option meaningfully de-risk SSRM, but with shares near 52-week highs, elevated 2026 costs and transaction-closing risk leave limited margin of safety.

IREN has a legitimate hyperscaler-backed AI cloud build-out, but the stock still carries too much dilution and execution risk to justify a fresh BUY while AI revenue remains early versus the capital required.
Cheap, cash-generative DSP with strong founder conviction and credible open-internet/CTV assets, but unresolved agency friction and soft ad verticals keep it in prove-it mode until May earnings.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: On April 7, unusual options flow showed 10,400 May 22, 2026 $200 puts trade at an average premium of $1.20, almost 18 times prior open interest, a structure Barchart read as bullish put-selling into Apple earnings expected on April 30.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Apple remains a high-quality cash machine with record Q1 results and near-term product/events catalysts, but at roughly 33x trailing earnings and less than 10% below its 52-week high, the stock already prices in a lot of the good news while Siri execution and tariff exposure remain open risks.
Cheap on forward earnings and helped by deleveraging, but the turnaround is still unproven after Coty withdrew FY26 guidance and guided to another weak quarter.

Kinsale remains a high-quality E&S underwriter with strong profitability and a near-term earnings catalyst, but the current setup looks fairly valued against softening property pricing and limited evidence of insider conviction buying.

Alphabet remains a high-quality cash compounder with real AI and cloud catalysts, but at roughly 24x forward earnings and only about 9% below its 52-week high, the setup looks more fairly valued than mispriced given capex and antitrust overhangs.
AWS and advertising keep Amazon fundamentally strong, but the stock is still expensive for a contrarian entry and 2026 capex/Kuiper spending raises the bar for upside.

Nebius has real AI-cloud demand and major 2026 capacity catalysts, but with the stock near its high, negative EBITDA/FCF, and capital-intensive execution still ahead, the setup is not attractive enough to chase.

Global Partners LP still throws off solid cash and keeps lifting its distribution, but high leverage, thin margins, limited upside, and mixed insider signals make the units unattractive for fresh capital here.
FDA approval of AVLAYAH de-risks Denali's platform, but after the approval catalyst the stock still needs to prove commercial uptake and confirmatory durability before it earns a fresh buy.

STERIS remains a high-quality sterilization and infection-prevention compounder, but tariff cost inflation, lingering EtO overhangs, and a still-full multiple keep the risk/reward only balanced into May earnings.

High-quality niche aerospace supplier with real aftermarket and acquisition-driven growth, but the stock still screens too expensive relative to its growth and now carries meaningfully higher debt after the LMB and Harper deals.

The planned automotive-industrial separation creates valuation upside, but current margin pressure, vendor-bankruptcy fallout, and execution risk keep GPC in hold territory at roughly 13x forward earnings.

McCormick is a defensively positioned flavor franchise, but after the March 31, 2026 Unilever Foods deal announcement the stock is now dominated by dilution, leverage, and integration risk rather than a clean value rerating.

Domino's remains a high-quality cash generator with an April 27, 2026 earnings catalyst, but mid-single-digit growth, weak international comps, and elevated leverage limit upside at the current price.

Strong Q4 earnings, locked-in VLCC charters, and a Q1 vessel-sale gain support near-term results, but FRO is already near highs and remains a leveraged bet on volatile tanker rates rather than a clear value buy.

Corcept has a fresh FDA-approved ovarian cancer launch and solid cash generation, but the hypercortisolism CRL and rising generic risk to Korlym keep the risk/reward from clearing a clean BUY bar yet.
Autonomy and robotaxi optionality are real, but Tesla's shrinking core auto economics and extreme valuation leave too little margin of safety.

catalysts_general: Discovery: The April 25, 2026 ban on short-term Russian LNG into Europe and Platts' exclusion of Russia-origin cargoes tighten Atlantic Basin replacement demand just as Venture Global is pushing higher Plaquemines throughput and signing long-term contracts.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Venture Global has real LNG ramp and financing momentum, but the stock does not compensate for extreme leverage, deeply negative free cash flow, and the unresolved BP arbitration damages overhang.
ARM has credible AI and data-center catalysts, but the stock still screens as materially overvalued versus near-term fundamentals and limited upside, so the risk/reward is unattractive here.

Interlink has some real new-program wins, but shrinking revenue, negative EBITDA, pending equity-plan dilution, and an acquisition-dependent story make the 71%-off-high setup look more like a value trap than a contrarian buy.
Mixed Phase 3 myelofibrosis data, ongoing dilution, and a cash runway only into late Q3 2026 leave KPTI too financing-dependent despite one more major endometrial catalyst ahead.
